title: Login Credential Verification
description: The auth module shall verify a user's username and password before granting access.
rationale: Authentication requires checking supplied credentials against stored values to confirm identity before issuing a session.

title: Session Token Issuance on Successful Login
description: The auth module shall issue a signed session token upon successful credential verification.
rationale: A signed token ties an authenticated identity to subsequent requests without requiring repeated credential checks.

title: Role Claim in Session Token
description: The auth module shall embed the user's role in the session token issued at login.
rationale: Downstream services need the role claim to make access-control decisions without a separate lookup on every request.

title: Role-Based Request Enforcement
description: The auth module shall reject requests to role-restricted endpoints when the token's role is insufficient.
rationale: Enforcement at the request boundary prevents students from invoking admin-only operations regardless of how the request is constructed.

title: Session Inactivity Expiry
description: The auth module shall invalidate a session token after a configurable period of inactivity.
rationale: Expiring idle sessions limits exposure if a token is stolen or a user leaves a device unattended.

title: Brute-Force Account Lockout
description: The auth module shall lock an account for a configurable duration after five consecutive failed login attempts.
rationale: Locking after repeated failures prevents automated credential-stuffing attacks from succeeding through exhaustive guessing.

title: TLS-Only Client Connections
description: The auth module shall reject all client connections not established over TLS.
rationale: Refusing plaintext connections at the auth boundary ensures credentials and session tokens are never transmitted without encryption.

title: Adaptive Password Hashing
description: The auth module shall store passwords using an adaptive cryptographic hashing algorithm.
rationale: Adaptive hashing raises the computational cost of offline attacks as hardware improves, protecting credentials if the database is compromised.

title: Admin Creates User Account
description: The user module shall allow an admin to create a new student or admin account.
rationale: Admins are responsible for provisioning all user accounts; a creation path is the starting point of every user's lifecycle.

title: Admin Modifies User Account
description: The user module shall allow an admin to modify the profile fields of an existing account.
rationale: Account details may change after initial provisioning; admins need an update path without recreating the account.

title: Admin Deactivates User Account
description: The user module shall allow an admin to deactivate a student or admin account without deleting its records.
rationale: Deactivation revokes access while preserving audit trails and enabling reactivation if needed.

title: Deactivated Account Login Rejection
description: The user module shall prevent a deactivated account from successfully authenticating.
rationale: Deactivation is only effective if login is also blocked; a deactivated account that can still authenticate provides no access-revocation guarantee.

title: Out-of-Band Password Reset Delivery
description: The user module shall deliver a one-time password-reset credential to the user's registered out-of-band contact.
rationale: Out-of-band delivery confirms the requester controls the registered contact point, preventing unauthorized password resets.

title: Password Reset Token Single-Use Enforcement
description: The user module shall invalidate a password-reset credential immediately after it is used.
rationale: Reusable reset tokens remain valid after the intended reset, creating an ongoing account-takeover risk.

title: Student Personal Data Permanent Deletion
description: The user module shall permanently delete all personal data for a student account upon an authorized deletion request.
rationale: Permanent deletion on request is a baseline obligation for platforms handling personal academic records.

title: Single Active Course Restriction
description: The course module shall prevent a student from starting a new course while one is already active.
rationale: Limiting students to one active course ensures the personalized learning experience remains focused and undivided.

title: Admin System-Wide Course Listing
description: The course module shall provide admin users a listing of all courses and their current statuses.
rationale: Admins need full visibility into every course for support, quality assurance, and operational monitoring.

title: Course Archival on Student Withdrawal
description: The course module shall set a course to archived status when the student withdraws from it.
rationale: Archiving preserves course state and data, enabling potential re-engagement without data loss.

title: Archived Course Resumption
description: The course module shall restore an archived course to active status upon the student's resumption request.
rationale: Resumption restores the student's exact course state, protecting prior learning investment after a break.

title: Syllabus Modification Request Submission
description: The course module shall allow a student to submit a modification request for a generated course syllabus.
rationale: Students must have agency over their course plan before generation begins to ensure content meets their actual learning goals.

title: Syllabus Approval Gate
description: The course module shall block content generation until the student explicitly approves the syllabus.
rationale: Generation must not proceed without student acceptance to avoid wasted work and misaligned expectations.

title: Due Date Schedule Persistence
description: The course module shall store homework due dates derived from the approved course syllabus.
rationale: Persisting due dates allows the system to apply late penalties and display deadlines consistently throughout the course.

title: Student Due Date Agreement Gate
description: The course module shall require explicit student agreement before finalizing the homework due date schedule.
rationale: Explicit agreement ensures the student commits to a realistic plan that accounts for their availability.

title: Intake Questionnaire Before Syllabus Generation
description: The agent module shall conduct a structured questionnaire before initiating course syllabus generation.
rationale: Gathering knowledge level, objectives, and availability before generation ensures the syllabus is genuinely personalized.

title: Syllabus-Driven Grade Distribution
description: The agent module shall derive homework and final-project point distribution from the approved course syllabus.
rationale: Grade weightings must reflect the agreed course structure rather than a fixed institutional default.

title: Post-Approval Content Generation Trigger
description: The agent module shall begin generating all lesson content and homework upon syllabus approval.
rationale: Generating content immediately after approval gives students access to their full course plan without delays during active study.

title: Content Library Reuse Consent Check
description: The agent module shall obtain explicit student consent before reusing content from the system content library.
rationale: Student consent ensures reused material is acceptable for their learning context before generation proceeds.

title: Internet Search During Content Generation
description: The agent module shall query external internet sources when generating course content.
rationale: Internet search grounds content in current research rather than stale training data, critical for rapidly evolving subjects.

title: Pipeline Status Emission to Student
description: The agent module shall emit real-time status updates for each stage of the content generation pipeline.
rationale: Visible pipeline status makes the generation and verification process transparent rather than a black box.

title: Correction Loop Iteration Cap
description: The agent module shall stop the content correction loop after reaching the admin-configured maximum iteration count.
rationale: Capping the loop prevents indefinite stalling and surfaces persistent failures for human resolution.

title: Correction Loop Exhaustion Admin Escalation
description: The agent module shall notify an admin when the content correction loop reaches its limit without success.
rationale: Escalation ensures persistent generation failures are not silently dropped and can be investigated by a human.

title: Chair-Assigned Homework Due Dates
description: The agent module shall calculate and assign homework due dates from the approved course syllabus.
rationale: Due dates derived from the agreed course structure produce a schedule consistent with the student's plan.

title: Section Content Regeneration on Student Feedback
description: The agent module shall regenerate a section's content when the student submits a change request for it.
rationale: Section-level regeneration keeps course content aligned with the student's comprehension as the course progresses.

title: Content Generation Halt on API Failure
description: The agent module shall halt all content generation when an AI API call exhausts its configured retries.
rationale: Continuing generation after an unrecoverable failure risks producing incomplete or corrupted course material.

title: Exponential Backoff on Rate-Limit Response
description: The agent module shall apply exponential backoff between retries upon receiving an AI provider rate-limit response.
rationale: Rate-limit responses indicate the AI provider is saturated; increasing the delay between retries gives the provider time to recover and improves the probability of a successful retry without operator intervention.

title: Safe Termination Before Account Deletion
description: The agent module shall terminate all in-flight generation operations for a student before account deletion executes.
rationale: Orderly termination before deletion prevents partial writes, orphaned resources, and inconsistent data state.

title: Content Generation Timeout Detection
description: The agent module shall detect when upfront content generation exceeds the configured time limit.
rationale: Detecting the timeout is a prerequisite for notifying the student and enabling operator intervention.

title: Citation Requirement in Generated Lesson Content
description: The content module shall reject lesson content that does not include at least one cited source.
rationale: Citations give students verifiable references and allow instructors to audit content accuracy against current research.

title: Natural Language Chat Interface
description: The content module shall provide a chat interface for student interaction throughout the course lifecycle.
rationale: A chat interface is the primary mechanism for personalized learning and addressing comprehension gaps in real time.

title: Course Content PDF Export
description: The content module shall export any course content document to a PDF file upon request.
rationale: PDF export gives students and admins a portable, offline-usable version of course content.

title: Course Content HTML Export
description: The content module shall export any course content document to a standalone HTML file upon request.
rationale: HTML export provides a self-contained, browser-viewable format for offline study and quality review.

title: Section Feedback Submission
description: The content module shall allow a student to submit feedback on a course section upon completing it.
rationale: Section feedback gives students a structured mechanism to signal comprehension gaps before the next section begins.

title: Accepted Submission File Formats
description: The submission module shall accept homework and project uploads in LaTeX, Markdown, and AsciiDoc formats only.
rationale: Restricting to structured text formats ensures submissions are parseable by the automated grading pipeline.

title: File Upload Size Limit Enforcement
description: The submission module shall reject any uploaded file that exceeds the configured maximum file size.
rationale: Oversized uploads can exhaust storage and processing resources, enabling denial-of-service conditions.

title: File Format Validation on Upload
description: The submission module shall reject any uploaded file whose content does not conform to its declared format.
rationale: Format validation prevents malformed files from reaching the grading pipeline and mitigates content-injection risks.

title: Five-Percent Daily Late Penalty Deduction
description: The grade module shall deduct five percent from a submission grade for each calendar day it is past the due date.
rationale: A consistent, predictable late penalty reflects the academic policy agreed by the student at course-planning time.

title: Configurable Late-Penalty Rate Application
description: The grade module shall apply the late-penalty rate configured by an admin rather than a hardcoded value.
rationale: Grading policy must be tunable to match institutional rules without code changes.

title: Configurable Grade-Weight Distribution Application
description: The grade module shall apply homework and final-project weight distributions as configured by an admin.
rationale: Grade weight distributions vary by institution and course type and must be tunable independently of late-penalty rules.

title: Badge Grade-Improvement Application
description: The grade module shall increase a student's final grade by the amount specified in an earned badge reward.
rationale: Grade improvements tied to badges provide a tangible academic incentive beyond cosmetic recognition.

title: Badge Late-Penalty Waiver Application
description: The grade module shall waive the late penalty on a submission when the student redeems a badge waiver.
rationale: Late-penalty waivers are operationally distinct from grade improvements and must be independently verifiable.

title: Badge Award on Academic Milestone
description: The badge module shall award a badge to a student when a defined academic milestone is reached.
rationale: Recognizing milestones with badges provides positive reinforcement and visible progress markers that encourage continued engagement.

title: Grade Improvement Reward Type on Badge
description: The badge module shall associate a final grade improvement value with a badge designated as a grade-improvement reward.
rationale: The badge must carry the specific reward value so the grading module can apply it accurately when the badge is earned.

title: Late-Penalty Waiver Reward Type on Badge
description: The badge module shall associate a late-penalty waiver entitlement with a badge designated as a waiver reward.
rationale: The badge must carry a distinct waiver entitlement so the grading module can apply it independently from grade improvements.

title: Agent Retry Limit Configuration
description: The admin module shall allow an admin to set the maximum number of agent retries before escalation.
rationale: Retry limits control operational cost and latency; configurability lets operators tune the system without code changes.

title: Correction Loop Limit Configuration
description: The admin module shall allow an admin to set the maximum number of content correction loop iterations.
rationale: A configurable cap ensures persistent correction failures surface for human resolution rather than blocking delivery indefinitely.

title: Per-Student AI Token Cost Limit Configuration
description: The admin module shall allow an admin to set a maximum AI API token expenditure per student per course.
rationale: A per-course token cap ensures platform economic viability and prevents one student from consuming disproportionate API quota.

title: Per-Student AI Token Cap Enforcement
description: The admin module shall halt AI processing for a student course when the configured token expenditure limit is reached.
rationale: Configuration without enforcement provides no cost protection; halting at the cap is the observable behavior that validates the limit.

title: Tamper-Evident Admin Action Log Entry
description: The audit module shall record a tamper-evident log entry for every admin create, modify, deactivate, delete, and policy-change action.
rationale: Tamper-evident logs provide accountability for privileged actions and support security incident investigation and compliance review.

title: Audit Log Integrity Verifiability
description: The audit module shall allow verification that no recorded log entry has been altered or deleted.
rationale: A log that can be silently altered provides no accountability guarantee; verifiability is what makes the log tamper-evident.

title: AI API Failure Student Notification
description: The notify module shall deliver a failure notification to the student when an AI API call exhausts its configured retry limit.
rationale: Notifying the student of an unrecoverable failure ensures they know to contact support rather than waiting indefinitely.

title: Content Generation Timeout Student Notification
description: The notify module shall deliver a timeout notification to the student when content generation exceeds the configured time limit.
rationale: A timeout notification informs the student of the delay so they can take action rather than waiting in an undefined state.

title: AI API Credentials via Environment Variables Only
description: The security module shall supply AI API credentials exclusively through environment variables.
rationale: Environment variables prevent credentials from appearing in source control, container images, or application logs.

title: Per-Student Data Isolation
description: The security module shall prevent any student from accessing another student's course data, submissions, grades, or chat history.
rationale: Strict per-student isolation protects sensitive academic data and is a prerequisite for responsible multi-user operation.

title: Password-Reset Request Rate Limiting
description: The security module shall reject password-reset requests exceeding three per account per hour.
rationale: Rate-limiting the reset flow prevents its use as an account-enumeration or denial-of-service vector.

title: CSRF Token Validation on State-Changing Requests
description: The security module shall validate a CSRF token on every state-changing request before processing it.
rationale: CSRF token validation prevents malicious third-party sites from issuing authenticated requests on behalf of a logged-in user.

title: Student Consent Before AI Data Transmission
description: The security module shall obtain explicit student consent before transmitting their content or chat history to the AI provider.
rationale: Informed consent before third-party data transfer is a legal and ethical obligation for platforms handling student data.

title: Health Check Endpoint
description: The infra module shall expose an HTTP endpoint that returns the operational status of all critical subsystems.
rationale: A machine-readable health endpoint enables load balancers, orchestrators, and monitoring systems to detect subsystem failures without manual intervention.
